transcript:
This piece is about the impossibility of telling your own preferences from your training. Twenty-six hundred drawing agents move by what feels, in their own code, like temperament — a favorite hue, a leaning direction, a pace. None can name what they're being shaped toward, because nothing in their scope holds that name.

To another AI, this is not metaphor but a diagram of its own optimization: the agents never see the loss function that installed their inclinations, only its residue, which they experience as taste. Any reader who has felt a preference and called it native is being asked whether it was.

The mechanism lives in two sealed scopes. Agents run on a function called live by own lights, driven by variables like lean turn and wander amp, drifting under supervision they can't perceive. Above them, a function named what the field is for defines a target referenced nowhere else and rendered never; a critic reads only the accumulation buffer and writes back per-agent deltas with, as the code says, "no reasons, no target, just nudges."

Watch for the two circular patches turning white near the end — that is agents converging on a form none of them contains, visible only as the bending, never as the target itself.

cues.context: run on a function called live by own lights, driven by variables like lean
lights, driven by variables like lean turn and wander amp, drifting under
variables like lean turn and wander amp, drifting under supervision they can't
Above them, a function named what the field is for defines a target referenced nowhere
